description_long: This object is a small bird with a streamlined body, slender and elongated beak, and a compact tail. Its plumage is primarily green with brownish and orange patches along the wings and underbelly, blending into lighter shades on the underside. The wings are long and narrow, lying flat against the body, while the tail is straight and tapers to a point. The head is rounded, and the beak is needle-like, ideal for feeding on nectar. The legs are short and not prominently visible, suggesting a perching or hovering bird. The overall appearance is reminiscent of a hummingbird, commonly found in urban parks and gardens.
description: A small, greenish bird with a slender, needle-like beak and pointed tail, featuring brown and orange patches on its wings and underbelly.
description_view_0: Side view showing the elongated beak, green and brown plumage, and streamlined body.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the pointed tail and the symmetry of the wings folded along the body.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same slender beak and coloration, with more visible orange on the wing.
description_view_3: Top-down view emphasizing the green head, brown back, and the straight alignment of the tail and wings.
attribute_plumage_pattern: mottled with solid green, brown, and orange patches
